summary: Phase 5 (Automated Seating + Inventory + Bulk-Import + Family-Move + Dev-Login) fully tested. 24/24 backend pytest tests pass. Frontend critical flows verified: Skip/Dev login button works and lands on /staff; Table Inventory tab + form present; Tables&Seating canvas shows ONLY inventory-driven palette (no legacy palette-table_round/rect/square); chairs render green for assigned + gray for empty; family inline table dropdown present on every guest row (18/18) with real options; bulk-import-btn opens modal with bulk-import-file input; auto-assign-btn opens preview modal. Over-capacity table indicator confirmed (Table #1 shows 14/10 seated).

minor.issue: tableNumber is typed as int (Pydantic); spec talks about table-types so non-numeric tableNumber returns 422. Minor UX inconsistency — table_number column is BIGINT/INT, but most plans use 'T-1', '101A' etc. Worth confirming with PM.

ui_bugs.issue: React hydration warning: '<span> cannot be a child of <option>'. Caused by the Emergent visual-editor instrumentation injecting wrapper <span> nodes into option labels. Functionally harmless (selects still work) but pollutes the console with red errors on every page load.

issues: Each table-type tile in the palette shows the full name (e.g. 'TEST_TT_1781271908_v2') which overflows the small tile width and gets clipped. Consider truncating with ellipsis or making the tile wider.

action_items: Decide whether tableNumber should accept strings (current model: int only). If yes, change TableInput.tableNumber to str and migrate DB column.
Truncate long table-type names in BallroomCanvas inventory palette tile so they don't visually clip.
Investigate the '<span> child of <option>' hydration warning — likely fix is to mark <option>{tableNumber}</option> as non-instrumented (or convert numeric children to plain text strings).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py L1638-1641: dead-store in move_family — `members = [g]` is immediately overwritten by the next assignment from the DB. Harmless but dead code, remove for clarity.
server.py L1330: openpyxl import inside the handler returns HTTPException(500) at request time if missing; cleaner to import once at module top and surface install errors at startup.

server.py L1344-1346: raw-body CSV branch raises 400 on empty body but doesn't validate content-type vs payload — a multipart upload with empty 'file' field falls into the else branch and tries to decode binary form data as CSV. Edge case but should be tightened.
BallroomCanvas inventory palette tile uses table-type name with no max-width / truncate utility — wraps/clips. Add `truncate w-full` to the label span.
auto-assign UI: 'Apply' button selector with text 'Apply' wasn't found; check button text consistency (the test used :has-text('Apply') and got null — may be 'Apply Plan' or icon-only).
test_credentials: Admin admin/admin123 (Supabase bootstrap) + Skip/Dev login button on /staff/login (DEV_AUTH_BYPASS=1).
seed_data_creation: test_phase5.py creates TEST_TT_<RUN_ID> table-type, FAM-<RUN_ID>-A/B/BIG families with TEST_-prefixed guests, and one round table on the first ballroom. Cleanup deletes the table and table-type at the end of the run (table-type delete returns 400 if any guest persisted assignment links to it — by design, since deletion is blocked when tables reference it). Residual TEST_ guests remain in Supabase from successful runs (visible as TEST_BigA/B/Smith/Jones/Excel rows in the Guest List screenshot).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All Phase-5 endpoints green: /api/auth/dev-login, /api/table-types CRUD, POST /api/tables with typeId (hydrates shape/seats/dims), POST /api/guests/bulk-import (raw CSV, multipart CSV, xlsx), POST /api/seating/auto-assign (apply true/false), POST /api/guests/family/move (with capacity 409). Frontend confirmed: skip-login-btn, tab-inventory, inventory-form, palette-tt-{id}, no legacy palette-table_* buttons, chair-*-green/gray rendering, over-capacity warning visible (Table #1 at 14/10), guest-table-select-{id} on every row with real table option strings, bulk-import-btn opens modal with bulk-import-file input, auto-assign-btn opens preview. Outstanding: cosmetic palette-tile truncation, React hydration warning re <span> inside <option> (visual-editor instrumentation), and tableNumber type debate (int vs str).
rca of the issue: No production bugs blocking phase-5 acceptance. Only cosmetic/console-noise findings documented above.
